Description: Median age at diagnosis 65-70 for most BM cancers; exponential increase after 55
Male-to-female ratio ~1.4:1 for most bone marrow cancers
Multiple myeloma incidence 2× higher in Black populations (SEER data)
First-degree relative with blood cancer increases risk 2-4×
Therapy-related AML/MDS accounts for 10-20% of cases; alkylating agents, topoisomerase II inhibitors
Occupational benzene exposure is an established leukemia risk factor (IARC Group 1 carcinogen)
Atomic bomb survivors, nuclear workers, medical radiation; dose-response relationship
Cigarette smoking increases AML risk ~40%; benzene in tobacco smoke
Obesity associated with chronic inflammation and altered cytokine profile
Rheumatoid arthritis, SLE, autoimmune hemolytic anemia increase risk
HIV patients have 2-3× risk of MM; HCV associated with MM and WM
Children with Down syndrome have 10-20× increased risk of acute leukemia
MGUS is a precursor condition; 1% per year progresses to myeloma
Agricultural workers with prolonged pesticide exposure show increased risk
Vietnam War veterans exposed to Agent Orange have elevated risk (VA recognized)
